channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Farke anticipates strong Chelsea performance despite managerial changes ahead of FA Cup semi-final
item.description: Leeds head coach Daniel Farke believes Chelsea will return to strong form after the club sacked head coach Liam Rosenior just four days before their FA Cup semi-final against Leeds. Rosenior’s dismiss...

item.title: China accelerates integration of AI into vehicles to reduce chip dependence and reshape the auto industry
item.description: China’s automotive sector is rapidly shifting its focus from electric vehicle dominance to the next major technological leap: embedding artificial intelligence directly into cars. Backed by Beijing’s ...

item.title: Boko Haram Suspected in Borno Attack: 20 Farmers and Loggers Killed
item.description: Omo, serious matter! At least 20 people, many of them farmers and firewood gatherers, don lost their lives for Borno State. The whole thing started with a landmine explosion, suspected to be the work ...

item.title: Eight Canadian Bars Ranked Among North America's 50 Best for 2026
item.description: The 2026 list of North America's 50 Best Bars has been revealed, with eight Canadian establishments making the prestigious ranking. Vancouver's Keefer Bar, known for its apothecary-inspired cocktails,...

item.title: Eastern Cape Premier Oscar Mabuyane launches defamation case against EFF leader Julius Malema over Fort Hare master’s degree allegations
item.description: Eastern Cape Premier Oscar Mabuyane has launched a defamation case in the high court against EFF leader Julius Malema over remarks Malema made publicly about Mabuyane’s master’s degree at the Universi...

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo scores her fourth UK number one with 'Drop Dead'
item.description: Olivia Rodrigo has achieved her fourth UK number one single with 'Drop Dead', the lead track from her upcoming third album, *You Seem Pretty Sad For A Girl So In Love*, due for release in June. The tr...

item.title: ICC names eight persons of interest linked to Duterte drug war; statuses as of 2026 outlined
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has identified eight individuals as alleged co-perpetrators in the case against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte over the controversial war on drugs. ...

item.title: MPs may vote on launching inquiry into Starmer over Mandelson vetting dispute
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer could face a House of Commons vote as early as next week on whether he should be investigated over his handling of the vetting process linked to Lord Peter Mandelson’s proposed appoin...

item.title: Six People Detained in Sopore, Kashmir Under PSA for Disrupting Student Protest
item.description: Authorities in Jammu and Kashmir have detained six individuals under the Public Safety Act (PSA) following disturbances during a student protest in Sopore. The protest had been organised in response t...

item.title: Man sentenced to life imprisonment for racially and religiously motivated assault and rape
item.description: A man, John Ashby, has received a life sentence for the brutal rape and religiously aggravated assault of a Sikh woman in her twenties in Walsall. During the attack, Ashby, who was homeless and recent...

item.title: Mounting pressure on Keir Starmer amid Mandelson row and party unrest
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has faced a deeply challenging week as political pressure within the Labour Party intensifies, largely driven by controversy surrounding Lord Mandelson’s security vetting and broader ...

item.title: Andrew Muller sworn in as ACT Supreme Court judge after decades of legal service
item.description: Justice Andrew Muller has been formally sworn in as a resident judge of the ACT Supreme Court, marking a significant milestone after more than 36 years working within the Canberra legal community. His...

item.title: Recto explains why travel authority is sometimes issued just a day before departure
item.description: Executive Secretary Ralph Recto clarified that it's not unusual for travel authorities for government officials to be issued just a day before their scheduled departure. This comes after Vice Presiden...

item.title: Dalung Warns of Instability in Tinubu's Inner Circle Following Edun's Exit
item.description: Solomon Dalung, wey be former Minister of Youth and Sports Development, don yarn say the way Wale Edun, wey don dey close to President Bola Tinubu for long, exit from government fit show say gbege dey...

item.title: Police pursue fourth suspect after seven family members killed in KwaDukuza attack
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal premier Thami Ntuli says it is only a matter of time before police arrest a fourth suspect linked to the brutal killing of seven members of the Monswamy family from KwaDukuza. The victim...

item.title: Arteta insists Arsenal still in Premier League race despite recent setbacks
item.description: Mikel Arteta don make am clear say Arsenal still get strong belief to win the Premier League title, even though dem don lose their top position to Manchester City. After City beat Burnley 1-0, both te...

item.title: Proposed Power Bill Changes Could Affect Household Battery Incentives
item.description: Australia's rapid growth in home batteries, which now accounts for 10% of new global capacity, faces potential disruption due to proposed changes to electricity network charges. The Australian Energy ...

item.title: Toronto Mother Missing Since 2024, Foul Play Suspected: Police Investigating
item.description: The disappearance of a Toronto mother, last seen at a Burlington home in 2024, is now being investigated as a possible case of foul play, according to local police. Authorities have not released speci...

item.title: IEC to Prosecute Voters Registered at Incorrect Addresses Ahead of Local Elections
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Africa (IEC) has issued a firm warning to all voters against registering with false addresses ahead of the local government elections. IEC Chairperson Mosotho Moepya,...

item.title: National Bank CEO Encourages Quebec to Develop Energy and Resource Infrastructure
item.description: Laurent Ferreira, CEO of National Bank of Canada, called on Quebec to accelerate development of its energy and natural resources to avoid falling behind other provinces in major infrastructure investm...
